design_philosophy.core_loop: Vertically-scrolling combat across prehistoric stages: navigate platforming hazards, destroy enemies, uncover power-ups from boulders, defeat stage bosses. Core tension between mobility management and weapon progression.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource scarcity of power-ups vs. enemy density and environmental hazards. Hitting enemies degrades weapon tier; death strips all upgrades. Players must balance offense with defensive movement.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Prehistoric warrior (Dino Riki) mastering elemental projectile attacks and mobility to dominate dinosaur-filled environments. Optional transformation into 'Macho Riki' (based on wrestler Riki Choshu in JP version) for ultimate power fantasy.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Uncompromising difficulty design paired with punishing degradation mechanics: single hit downgrade weapon tier; falling water kills instantly. No difficulty settings. Game was explicitly repositioned mid-development from action-exploration (arrow-based screen navigation, flower collection, 16 stages) to brutal vertically-scrolling shooter to match Konami's Knightmare template. Demands pattern memorization and precise platforming within shmup constraints.

difficulty.lives_system: Multiple lives with persistent loss on death; specific checkpoints reset player to stage position with all power-ups removed
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based checkpoints. Falling into water results in instant death. Taking hits downgrades weapon tier progressively (rocks → axes → boomerangs → torches). Death resets all upgrades.
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Minimal. No selectable difficulty modes documented. Progression solely through skill mastery and power-up discovery. Some environmental hazard telegraphing (moving lilypads, submerging platforms) allows skilled navigation.

progression.upgrade_system: Tiered weapon progression: rocks (base) → stone axes → boomerangs → torches, each improving range and spread. Speed boosts and flight-enabling wings as temporary power-ups. Hidden 'Macho Riki' transformation for powerful clone-firing attack. Weapon downgrades on hit; complete reset on death.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Power-ups discovered by destroying boulders scattered throughout stages. Final hidden transformation requires collecting specific rare icon. Four main stages with last stage containing four sub-stages.
replayability_hooks: High difficulty naturally encourages repeated attempts and skill refinement
Hidden power-up locations reward exploration within stage design
Macho Riki transformation discovery provides goal for replay
Pattern memorization of boss patterns and enemy spawns enables speedrunning optimization

world_building_techniques: Prehistoric setting with dinosaur enemies and environmental obstacles (water hazards, lilypads)
Platforming sections contrast with shoot-em-up action, grounding player in physical world
Character transformation (Macho Riki) tied to culturally-specific persona (wrestler Choshu in JP, Master Higgins archetype in US)
Minimal explicit narrative; world communicated through visual theme and obstacle design

boss_design.philosophy: Stage-terminating encounters requiring pattern recognition and projectile avoidance. Bosses serve as difficulty spikes validating player skill accumulation. Boss victory includes dedicated jingle (composed by Takeaki Kunimoto).
notable_bosses: Stage bosses (4 stages total, final stage has 4 sub-stage bosses)
Specific boss identities not documented in sources; pattern-based design inferred from scrolling shooter genre conventions

faction_indicators: Japanese release version features wrestler Riki Choshu as cultural celebrity endorsement and transformation form, signaling appeal to Japanese wrestling fandom
US release rebrands Macho transformation to Master Higgins archetype, indicating regional audience segmentation
danger_signals: Unforgiving instant-death water hazards create tension and visible player failure states
Weapon degradation on hit creates visible downgrade moments, signaling vulnerability
Visible health/score/lives display at top of screen emphasizes mortality and resource depletion
environmental_storytelling: Prehistoric landscape filled with dinosaurs establishes primitive-world setting
Environmental hazards (water bodies, lilypads, submerging platforms) communicate danger through world design rather than explicit narrative
Boulder destruction mechanic grounds power-up discovery in physical interaction with environment

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Adventures of Dino Riki (regional differences, transformation mechanics, difficulty characterization)
Wikipedia - Adventures of Dino Riki (gameplay loop, development history, design philosophy shifts, boss structure, power-up system, checkpointing, staff credits)
